title: What Makes Humans Stupid
gist: In an essay, David Krakauer argues stupidity is a sophisticated behavior requiring intelligence, noting that a species capable of Mars rovers and genome sequencing simultaneously sustains conspiracy theories and anti-scientific movements.

insights.claim: David Krakauer argues stupidity is a sophisticated form of behavior that requires intelligence, noting rocks, rivers, hurricanes, and thermostats are never described as stupid.
insights.speaker: David C. Krakauer
insights.so_what: Reframes stupidity as an emergent property of intelligent systems, not its absence.
insights.quote: It takes intelligence to get things spectacularly wrong.
insights.locator: I have never heard a rock

insights.claim: The same humans who land autonomous rovers on Mars, sequence genomes in hours, and engineer nanometer circuits also sustain proliferating conspiracy theories, anti-scientific political movements, and institutional hatred.
insights.speaker: David C. Krakauer
insights.so_what: Highlights the coexistence of high competence and mass irrationality.
insights.locator: Human beings can land autonomous rovers
